title: Polymer clay face bead
subtitle: A sculpted face on a bead form, built up from small applied pieces
excerpt: A polymer clay bead with a sculpted face: eyes, nose, and lips built up in layers from small applied clay pieces. Baked at 110-130°C. Suitable for jewellery, keyrings, or hanging decoration. Intermediate difficulty because of the small-scale sculpting.
sourceNotes: Polymer clay face bead construction derived from first principles.

glossaryTerms.definition: PVC-based modelling material that sets in a domestic oven at 110-130°C. Decorative use only.
The heat process that sets polymer clay in a domestic oven.

content.text: is built by adding small pieces of clay onto a bead core and pressing them into face features. The proportions of a face are simpler than they seem: the eyes sit on the horizontal midline of the head; the nose base sits halfway between eyes and chin; the mouth sits one-third of the way from nose to chin. Getting these proportions right is what makes a face bead readable.

attrs.title: Baking polymer clay
attrs.body: Use a dedicated baking tray. Verify oven temperature with a thermometer. Do not share with food cookware.

items.name: Polymer clay, skin tone
items.substitution: Mix white and a touch of terracotta for a basic skin tone

items.name: Polymer clay, accent colour (for eyes, lips)

content.text: Roll 15 g of skin-tone clay into a smooth oval about 25 mm tall and 20 mm wide. Push a skewer through the bead vertically from top to bottom; this forms the bead hole and allows the bead to hang on a skewer during baking, which keeps it round.

Roll a tiny ball of skin-tone clay (about 4 mm diameter) and press it onto the front face of the bead at the midpoint between the equator and the bottom of the oval. Blend the sides of the nose into the face with a needle tool, leaving the tip round and raised.

Roll two very small balls of white clay (2 mm diameter) and press them onto the face equator, one on each side of the nose with about 6 mm between them. Press each flat against the face. Add a smaller dark clay dot on each white eye. Add a tiny flat piece of skin-tone clay above each eye as an eyelid line.
Roll a small cylinder of lip-colour clay (about 10 mm long and 3 mm wide) and press it onto the lower face. Use a needle tool to indent a line across the middle dividing upper and lower lip.
Rest the skewer across two blocks so the bead hangs freely. Bake at 110°C (fan) for 20 minutes.
suspended on the skewer prevents one face of the bead from flattening on the tray.

Once cooled, paint details with a fine liner brush and acrylic paint if wanted: eyelashes, freckles, flush on the cheeks. Seal with a coat of polymer clay gloss. Thread onto a cord, key ring, or jump ring.

items.cause: Applied pieces not pressed firmly enough onto the base
items.fix: Press each applied piece firmly so it bonds; smooth the join edges into the surrounding clay

items.cause: Bead rested on the tray during baking
items.fix: Always bake suspended on a skewer resting across two blocks
